Free Summer Fun — Central Lake County, IL ☕ Buy us a coffee

Let's give our kids
a 1990s summer.

Bike rides 'til the streetlights. Splash pads on Tuesdays. Library storytime on Mondays. Firefly walks on Fridays. A field guide to the free, close-to-home, outside summer your kids deserve.

$

Free, mostly.

Every event is free or a few bucks. We mark the ones that cost money in red.

↻

Close to home.

All within 45 minutes of Grayslake. Most within 20. Built for the way little kids actually travel.

☀

Outside, ideally.

Sun on faces, grass on knees. The library backup plan exists, but we lead with the prairie.

★

For little kids.

Ages 0 to 8, with notes on what's stroller-friendly, what needs a kid who can read, and what's worth the nap-skip.

🚲 Ride your bike 📚 Library storytime 🌊 Splash pads 🌿 Nature walks 🎨 Free crafts 🛝 Playground hop 🦋 Butterfly houses 🌽 Farm visits 🚲 Ride your bike 📚 Library storytime 🌊 Splash pads 🌿 Nature walks 🎨 Free crafts 🛝 Playground hop 🦋 Butterfly houses 🌽 Farm visits

Events were gathered using web scraping and automated tools — details may not be complete or fully accurate. Always confirm times, registration, and cost directly with the organizer before you go.

If this has been useful to you, we'd love it if you bought us a coffee — or shared it with another parent.

Other Free Fun

Museum Adventure Pass

Libraries & Museums Near You

Library (has MAP passes) Museum (accepts MAP passes) Free admission venue

Check out MAP passes at any participating library with your library card. museumadventure.org

Illinois Library Program

Museum Adventure Pass

Got a Lake County library card? You can check out free or discounted admission passes to museums and cultural venues across Illinois — including the Chicago Botanic Garden, Illinois Railway Museum, and more.

  • Check out passes in person or request online
  • 7-day loan period — print or show on your phone
  • One pass per family, first-come first-served
  • Works with any participating Illinois library card
Browse Passes at museumadventure.org
Participating Libraries
Grayslake Area PL
Cook Memorial PL
Warren-Newport PL
Fox Lake District PL
Lake Villa District PL
Wauconda Area PL
Round Lake Area PL
Waukegan PL
Fremont PL
Vernon Area PL
Check museumadventure.org to confirm your library participates.
Round Lake · Every Wednesday

Children's Neighborhood Museum

Free admission every Wednesday, 12:30–2:30 PM. Located in the lower level of the Robert W. Rolek Community Center in Round Lake.

814 Hart Road, Round Lake, IL 60073 Wednesdays 12:30–2:30 PM Free
Illinois State Senate · District 30

Senator Edly-Allen's Museum Pass

Senator Mary Edly-Allen's office offers a free Museums in the Park pass to constituents in her district. The pass provides complimentary general admission to participating Chicagoland museums.

You must live in Illinois Senate District 30 (Lake Forest, Lake Bluff, Highwood, and surrounding communities). Her office will confirm eligibility.

Participating museums include:
Adler Planetarium · Art Institute of Chicago · Brookfield Zoo · Chicago Botanic Garden · Chicago Children's Museum · Chicago History Museum · DuSable Black History Museum · The Field Museum · Illinois Holocaust Museum · Lincoln Park Zoo · Museum of Science and Industry · Peggy Notebaert Nature Museum · and more
Request Senator Edly-Allen's Pass
Illinois State Representative

Rep. Laura Faver Dias's Museum Pass

Representative Laura Faver Dias's office also offers a Museums in the Park pass for constituents — complimentary general admission to the same great Chicagoland museums.

You must live in Rep. Faver Dias's district. Her office will confirm eligibility before coordinating pass pickup.

Request Rep. Faver Dias's Pass

Free Summer Fun
★ Made for Lake County kids · Vol. I · No. 03 ★ Central Lake County, IL · Summer 2026 · About & Terms

About & Terms of Use

What this is. Free Summer Fun is a personal, volunteer-built guide to free and low-cost summer events and outdoor spots for families with young kids in central Lake County, Illinois. It is not a for-profit endeavor, has no affiliation with the libraries, park districts, forest preserves, or municipalities listed, and earns no money from the listings.

Use at your own risk. Events, times, locations, prices, registration requirements, and program availability change frequently. Listings are aggregated from public calendars and may contain errors, omissions, or out-of-date information. Always confirm details directly with the organizer before traveling to or relying on any event. We make no warranty, express or implied, about accuracy, completeness, fitness for a particular purpose, or safety of any activity, location, or organization listed. You are solely responsible for your own and your children's safety, supervision, and decisions to participate.

No endorsement. Inclusion of an organization, event, or park does not constitute endorsement. Logos, names, and trademarks remain the property of their respective owners.

Accessibility. Reasonable efforts have been made to design and build this site to meet WCAG 2.1 Level AAA conformance and the spirit of the Americans with Disabilities Act, including 7:1 text contrast, keyboard navigation, semantic landmarks, and respect for reduced-motion preferences. Accessibility is an ongoing effort — if you encounter a barrier, please email so it can be fixed.

Privacy. This site sets no tracking cookies, runs no analytics, and collects no personal information. The "bucket list" check-offs are stored only in your own browser's local storage.

Contact & corrections. Spotted something wrong or missing? Reach out via the email link associated with this site so the listing can be updated.

Last updated Summer 2026 · Provided as-is, with love, for the neighborhood.
